title: Yoga as a therapeutic approach to mental health in university students: a randomized controlled trial
headline: Yoga as a therapeutic approach to mental health in university students: a randomized controlled trial
summary: A randomized controlled trial of yoga examined emotional wellbeing. The purpose of this study has been to analyze the efficacy of a yoga-based intervention on stress, emotional wellbeing, state anxiety and trait anxiety in university students.
keyFindings: The purpose of this study has been to analyze the efficacy of a yoga-based intervention on stress, emotional wellbeing, state anxiety and trait anxiety in university students.
This study was a randomized controlled trial.
limitations: Single-trial result; requires replication, and self-reported outcomes may inflate apparent effects.
The practice studied (yoga) is not the Miracle of Mind practice; findings do not transfer directly to MoM.
simplifiedTitle: Yoga as a therapeutic approach to mental health in university students: a randomized controlled trial
aiSnippet: RCT, Frontiers in public health (2024). The purpose of this study has been to analyze the efficacy of a yoga-based intervention on stress, emotional wellbeing, state anxiety and trait anxiety in university students. This study was a randomized controlled trial.
mechanismGap: The practice in this study does not share a specific breath-regulation or directed-attention mechanism with the Miracle of Mind practice, so no practice bridge is drawn here.
